mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ months, equity risk premiums expand as real discount rates rise, compressing present values of future corporate earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 5.26% indicates restrictive monetary stance
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated real rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, driving positive equity returns as forward earnings expectations rise. This is an always-on trend signal independent of regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns in > 55% of non-recession months.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, consumer demand destruction signals earnings headwinds. The joint deterioration in both demand and labor conditions predicts negative SPX returns over 60 trading days as forward guidance revisions cascade.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines MoM AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same month
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 367,539M and UNRATE at 4.4%
regime_hint: late-cycle contraction or post-shock demand collapse
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 5-year training window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month, signaling supply-side weakness and potential margin compression. This supply constraint predicts negative equity returns over 5 trading days as near-term earnings revisions incorporate production shortfalls.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances when INDPRO growth < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 1,600 thousand units annualized while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains positive, signaling sustained demand-side momentum and forward consumer wealth effects. This dual demand confirmation predicts positive SPX returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1,600 thousand units AND GDPC1 growth > 1.5% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1,525 thousand units, GDPC1 at 11,513B
regime_hint: sustained expansion with healthy demand dynamics
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) decelerates to below 3.0% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5%, indicating the Fed has successfully tightened into disinflation. This regime transition signals imminent rate cuts, compressing discount rates and producing positive SPX returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 203.906 index, FEDFUNDS at 5.26%
regime_hint: monetary policy pivot from tightening to easing cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when disinflation signal is triggered, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.5% in a single month, signaling labor market deterioration and forward earnings risk from reduced consumer spending. This threshold crossing predicts negative SPX returns over 20 trading days as equity risk premiums expand.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.4%, near trigger threshold
regime_hint: labor market softening or early-cycle slowdown
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates to below 1.0% month-over-month while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,400 thousand units, indicating synchronized demand weakness across consumer and housing sectors. This dual demand collapse predicts negative SPX returns over 60 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth < 1.0% AND HOUST < 1,400 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 367,539M, HOUST at 1,525 thousand units
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 5-year window.
